name: MUST-SEE (via DSCC)
subject: President Obama is asking you, team
disclaimer_text: paid for by cutting health care for everybody else
body:
Did you see what Barack Obama said about the Senate GOP's health care repeal bill?
---
**[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170625-MustSeeDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team,

Did you see what Barack Obama said about the GOP health care repeal bill? He said:

> _" The Senate bill, unveiled today, is not a health care bill. It's a massive transfer of wealth from middle-class and poor families to the richest people in America. It hands enormous tax cuts to the rich and to the drug and insurance industries, paid for by cutting health care for everybody else. Those with private insurance will experience higher premiums and higher deductibles, with lower tax credits to help working families cover the costs, even as their plans might no longer cover pregnancy, mental health care, or expensive prescriptions. Discrimination based on pre-existing conditions could become the norm again. Millions of families will lose coverage entirely._

_**"Simply put, if there 's a chance you might get sick, get old, or start a family -- this bill will do you harm."**_
Then, he called on all of us to "speak out" and make sure Republican senators know what impact this bill will have on our families.

[**We 're running ads in key states showing what this bill will cost working Americans and with a vote likely this week, we urgently need your help to up the pressure. Chip in $1 right now to help call out the Republican plan and stop this disastrous repeal bill.**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170625-MustSeeDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

When President Obama left office, he said he'd weigh in on politics only when the stakes were truly high and the moment demanded he speak out.

This is one of those moments, team. Republicans are determined to hold a vote by the end of next week, and if the bill passes, millions of Americans will lose the access to affordable health care we fought so hard for under President Obama.

**We 've come too far to let all our progress slip away now. The next week is critical, so please chip in $1 right now to help fund our ads and turn the pressure up on Republicans to oppose this bill:**

[**http://dscc.org/it-is-time-to-save-obamacare**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170625-MustSeeDS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e479/2b07114c/26b2555e/73103908/3461788853/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e479/2b07114c/26b2555e/7310390b/3461788853/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e479/2b07114c/26b2555e/7310390a/3461788853/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). name: Tim Kaine
subject: re: left behind
body:
Say you're ready to elect Democrats. **Pitch in $3 or more if you're ready to elect Democrats -- we can't do this without you:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523345/4067b0a/35291d58a/7e68b37a/1538609503/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Elect-Democrats-2017-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170626_tk_nd&source=em_20170626_tk_nd&refcode=em_20170626_tk_nd")****
Derek, there's a simple fact about our party: Democratic values are American values.

So when Donald Trump is in power, it's the voters who get left behind.

We're seeing it every day in Washington and statehouses around the country. Taking health care away from millions of people, backing out of the Paris Climate Accord, cutting critical services like Meals on Wheels and winter heating assistance -- this isn't what most Americans voted for.

**[If we're going to get our country back on the right path, we have to do everything we can to win. And that means we have to get started right now.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523345/4067b0a/35291d58a/7e68b37b/1538609503/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/Elect-Democrats-2017-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+If+were+going+to+get+our+country+back+on&utm_campaign=em_20170626_tk_nd&source=em_20170626_tk_nd&refcode=em_20170626_tk_nd")**

There are important elections this year in Virginia and New Jersey, and then the race to take back our majorities in Congress will be in full swing.

I'm ready to fight back against President Trump's agenda, Derek. If you are too, pitch in $3 or whatever you can to stand with Democrats.

name: The White House
subject: Your 1600 Daily
body:
1600 Daily Everything White House for 6/26/2017. |
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f0791304af940ec0ca66b5e010e34bcf0c5e1ba24f7296f6d173611cffde7045d6b92061619c875fd556298bf1231d3c05e

Obamacare's record speaks for itself. Premiums continue to rise, millions remain uninsured, and insurance providers are fleeing the market faster than ever. Obamacare is failing the American people. President Donald J. Trump remains committed to working with Congress to repeal and replace the disaster that is Obamacare.

Today's Events

10:30 AM: President Trump receives his daily intelligence briefing

1:00 PM: President Trump has lunch with Vice President Mike Pence and U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Nikki Haley

1:30 PM: Press Briefing with Press Secretary Sean Spicer

2:30 PM: Vice President Pence joins Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services Administrator Seema Verma for a listening session with victims of Obamacare -
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f074a2d6d727ab7cf97096f36a3078d44a17b0037e47abf846f485086c91925e6c033eeb4bffdbb692b35850abdb3687805
Watch

3:35 PM: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump welcome Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India

4:15 PM: President Trump leads an expanded bilateral meeting with Prime Minister Modi

5:15 PM: President Trump gives joint statements with Prime Minister Modi -
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07d8f5687d8db4c64d3e14b776585c06e6e5f3f12771359463884962dbc04898ff84ea945aee481e788a5f7460fb28405b
Watch

6:00 PM: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump have dinner with Prime Minister Modi

7:30 PM: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump participate in the departure of Prime Minister Modi

Photo of the Day

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f0788c4c5ece9391c9ee68df26ba8c44c385dc180f2a77b61d03119d3c7dc45defec75af98599f01fca4fe8cf51e0d375fc

President Donald J. Trump signs the Department of Veterans Affairs Accountability and Whistleblower Protection Act of 2017 | June 23, 2017

OBAMACARE FACT OF THE DAY

FACT: When Obamacare was signed into law, CBO estimated that 23 million people would be covered in Obamacare's exchanges in 2017.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07d12fe0413de5d225f53461c2fe445db0064a8e81e6d7819d70b101df23e39984a1d1bf4a09f5c51673c8aab139549e10
They were off by more than 100% - only 10.3 million people are covered by Obamacare.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07d12fe0413de5d225f53461c2fe445db0064a8e81e6d7819d70b101df23e39984a1d1bf4a09f5c51673c8aab139549e10

WHITE HOUSE UPDATES

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f078efbda38e0a61b11d741b4487f9b48aada8711c56be8a11a4dc51387871b5e396265edccc3d903c07472ff079d3c1dea

On Friday, President Donald J. Trump
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f0756b9c731d27c358b07ed3d8446938a2333b821976288ca153c1c3ce1d59627e31534318d224b8bac88a80e6685a84590
signed the Department of Veteran Affairs Accountability and Whistleblower Protection Act of 2017, which will bring accountability back to the VA. This bill gives senior officials in the Department of Veterans Affairs the power to fire failing employees and establishes safeguards to protect those who reveal wrongdoing in the agency.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f0735802f1a38b9affe79413e5fa1d3e2411a6aba212cec89e4b06757f785bdcf82f0853bc4485b914a7584268980fbcc36

On Friday, Vice President Pence
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07f9f4e1323d1a9a40199456cf7d4790e92e24ebf56977e6b8a99ecd632f3839fd4929bec8b7ac60daaa71232d21874476
gave remarks at the Focus on the Family 40th Anniversary Celebration in Colorado Springs, Colorado. He congratulated the organization on their historic milestone and praised their efforts to strengthen the American Family.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07b565ca278d89657ea522856813c0e8e3e4a842fd6d203be2ea17fd5aeb157cf8037319f784486e50bedff095088aba8d

Last week, President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07f611881a8e0e5946e464529fd71477861426ec800a8c854c5230a27a0c975d089e307f0198817fd5952828ea76298774
hosted Congressional members and their families on the South Lawn for the annual Congressional Picnic.

West Wing Reads

In the
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f077bbc25dbe650455b7566d5f0067fdccc27584f0d5ac51ded511e57e40742ea4cb66ea73737624cb0974bbd161054ab1b
Philadelphia Inquirer , Sen. Pat Toomey (R) praises the Senate repeal bill, writing that it “will help to drive down costs for everyone” and fulfill the promise to repeal Obamacare.

------------

Today starts energy week at the White House.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07378803d6a8bac31d2f50ec3e5c6ec0abcea4038f70a4d76289d33e56aaef5da7f2a827e55f79ea187ccfea1f5ce83751
Bloomberg previews the week, particularly the President’s message of American “energy dominance” through increased exports. And
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07f4383fce7c9c6dcaad2e405853837036576d8d6a136c175b0ba5abc799cebc88412103e538be6d49dd463533d29fe5d1
The Wall Street Journal reports on the ripple effect the U.S. shale energy boom has had on the plastics and chemicals industries, including supporting jobs, increasing exports, and further supporting the U.S. economy.

------------

In
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f0755006c17fac1365bc6e54410e4c65041bad9a3f53b65e7e0f29091cf748f5462889336ecc0d8ff94a77488763c9feb79
The Hill , former Reagan and Bush 43 Transportation Secretaries Norman Mineta and James Burnley write that now is the time to privatize the air traffic control system – a key initiative the President supports and that kicked off our infrastructure week earlier this month.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07717b403aead578a4b4431514c1c440a49b6be31a1b64922e51f7c102e07c355e837af1ef1f696237933a592519131534

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f07103fb2e0b35aad5f8c1f71baf9d3df8f26f4e8b3cf4a429b25f6fb5c5289ebe682c0d5fce8551e83927f28497d96b988
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=680df6434f7c3f0764e56078905773ccd7d8988967da770b2d7dd8c19bc735fa7f596908f765a9ab0573e538fdcc330b8af287078ac8ddd0
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4bb3b20baf2bc942aedb3206e86e3d02602b8d5921e25274b32d4617c1ecd9982bd908435686f117b31650efa7cf159b0
Unsubscribe

subject: We need your thoughts. (healthcare)

body:

| | Peter,

In May, House Republicans passed their "American Health Care Act," which would repeal key provisions of the Affordable Care Act and endanger health care for millions of Americans. Now, Senate Republicans are looking to pass their own health care bill -- one that will gut Medicaid and raise premiums for Hoosiers by thousands of dollars.

One of the most concerning issues with the House and Senate bills is that both could allow insurance companies to go back to doing what they did before the passage of the ACA -- charging people with pre-existing conditions more for their health care coverage.

Health experts estimate that one in four Americans has a pre-existing condition. **In Indiana, that 's over 1.6 million people who could be affected**. And that's to say nothing about the other, equally alarming aspects of these bills -- like the fact that it would kick _hundreds of thousands_ of Hoosiers off of HIP 2.0.

**Joe wants to hear from you:**

Do you support House and Senate Republicans' plans to repeal and replace the ACA?

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Derek, here's what health care repeal would mean for women:
body:
Pitch in before our end-of-quarter deadline. **Pitch in $3 or more before our end-of-quarter deadline to help elect Democrats all across the country.
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523350/4067b0a/35148bff2/7e68ad4c/3197885163/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170626_mh_nd&source=em_20170626_mh_nd&refcode=em_20170626_mh_nd")****
Derek --

Last week, just like you, I finally got to see the health care repeal bill that Senate Republicans have been working on in secret.

I can't say I'm surprised that 13 Republican men came up with a bill that would be disastrous for millions of American women.

Now, they're rushing their bill to a vote -- and we must stop it from passing. **[Can you pitch in $3 or whatever you can to stand with my Democratic colleagues and all the Democrats across the country who are fighting back against health care repeal?](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523350/4067b0a/35148bff2/7e68ad4d/3197885163/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Can+you+pitch+in+3+or+whatever+you+can+t&utm_campaign=em_20170626_mh_nd&source=em_20170626_mh_nd&refcode=em_20170626_mh_nd")**

The health care options that are available to women should be equal to what's available to men. That means basic health care needs -- like preventive care, cancer screenings, maternity care, and, yes, the constitutionally-protected right to access abortion services -- should be covered.

**[That won't happen if the GOP's health care repeal becomes law.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523350/4067b0a/35148bff2/7e68ad4a/3197885163/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+That+wont+happen+if+the+GOPs+health+care&utm_campaign=em_20170626_mh_nd&source=em_20170626_mh_nd&refcode=em_20170626_mh_nd")**

If Republicans get their way, insurers would no longer be required to cover essential health benefits like maternity care.

The GOP's draconian cuts to Medicaid would disproportionately affect women. Medicaid funding pays for nearly one-half of births nationwide, and in my state of New Hampshire, 62 percent of Medicaid recipients are women.

And it wouldn't be a Republican health care bill if it didn't defund Planned Parenthood -- which doesn't only affect women's reproductive health choices, Derek. Millions of women across the country get their primary and preventive care from Planned Parenthood. In some communities, Planned Parenthood is the only women's health care provider available. Defunding it would cut off access to routine checkups, mammograms, and cancer screenings for hundreds of thousands of American women.

When it comes right down to it, there's only one way to look at the GOP's health care repeal: they're playing political games with every woman in this country's right to make her own health care decisions. We have to fight back with everything we've got.

Chip in $3 or more to stand together with Democrats and elect leaders who will protect our health care.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Why I'm vetoing the budget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Because this budget shortchanges the needs of our growing state and has the wrong priorities, I will veto it. Add your name here if you have my back.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202c90/2fde7c66/4286c728/15697f83/3234830764/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/let-roy-know-you-have-his-back-on-the-budget?utm_campaign=em17_170626_budget_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_170626_budget_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")
---
Friends,

In my first six months in office, I've met all kinds of North Carolinians.

I've met people ready to turn big ideas into small businesses. I've met teachers who reach into their own pockets to buy school supplies for their students. I've met middle-class families who are trying to get ahead but just aren't feeling the effects of a recovering economy. I've seen whole communities struggling to deal with the grip of opioid addiction.

We need a budget that enables the big dreams of our people and meets the needs of our state. Unfortunately, the budget passed by the legislature last week is not that budget.

We don't need a budget that shortchanges public education and our teachers. We don't need a massive tax giveaway to millionaires and corporations at the expense of middle-class families. We can't afford to let the opioid crisis devastate our communities.

**[Because this budget shortchanges the needs of our growing state and has the wrong priorities, I will veto it. name: The Sanders Institute
subject: The future of our healthcare
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friends,
We all understand that healthcare in the United States is complicated. Why is it that every other industrialized country has a universal healthcare system while the US has yet to find a solution that works for the many? Why is it that we spend far more per capita on healthcare but still leave millions without any coverage?
**[Part of our mission at The Sanders Institute is to explore bold solutions to the pressing issues that face our country; like Medicare for All. ](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0dd7/43f17890/cbde0040/62035411/144415035/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/medicare-for-all-vs-all-the-healthcare-that-each-can-afford")**
This past week, the Senate began debating the American Health Care Act (AHCA) with a vote on that bill expected to take place later this week. The Congressional Budget Office estimates that **14 million more people would be uninsured in 2018** under the new plan than under current law; and by 2026 the number of uninsured would reach 51 million Americans. While the Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) has made strides to cover more Americans, it is estimated that 28 million would still not have health insurance in 2026. Either way, we have a long way to go.
Many believe that it is time to explore other options when it comes to how we provide healthcare in the United States. In a joint effort, National Nurses United and The Sanders Institute have published a paper that speaks to the current state of healthcare in the US and explores the option of a Medicare-for-all single payer system.
[_**Medicare for All vs. All the Healthcare That Each Can Afford**_](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0dd7/43f17890/cbde0040/62035411/144415035/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/medicare-for-all-vs-all-the-healthcare-that-each-can-afford")
Thank you for staying engaged,
The Sanders Institute

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: earn it every day
body:
#
Team --
Since the beginning of this campaign, we've known that our only path to victory is one we're willing to travel together. This isn't my campaign for Governor -- it's our campaign. And that's why I want to talk with you all today about our long-term campaign strategy -- because, as a member of our team, you deserve to know what our plan looks like.
Our first election challenge will be our primary, which is a little over a year from today, in August 2018. By this time next June, we'll need to have a solid base of volunteers and organizers across the state. Our goal is to make sure we have people regularly calling voters and knocking doors in every single one of Florida's 67 counties. When I was an organizer in college, I used a method that we called "Arrive with Five" \-- where we'd encourage people to not only show up to vote, but to make sure they show up with five of their friends, too. That's a strategy (and a good tradition) that I would like to continue with our campaign.
We're going to be up against self-funders, well-known names, and a lot of cynics and critics. And that's one of the reasons why we're starting these efforts so early -- because it takes time to build a grassroots movement and a ground game that can take on the special interests. There are no shortcuts -- this is a victory that we are going to have to earn every single day over the next year. This is a victory we cannot achieve alone.
[**And that 's why we're asking: can we count on you to make a grassroots contribution to sustain our campaign through the election?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3313/9e00690/4d24f1b8/24037aaf/1749865139/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170626-nb-nr")
Before we start opening satellite campaign offices across the state, we need to know that we'll consistently have the resources we need to pay rent, purchase supplies like water and snacks for volunteers, and print campaign literature for us to hand out at the doors. We're going to run a lean and smart campaign -- please try not to tease us for our plastic picnic-table desks -- but it will still be expensive to reach out to as many voters as we need to in a state as big as Florida.
On this campaign, there isn't any secret strategy to focus on one group of Floridians based on where they live or what they look like. We're going to run this campaign in all 67 counties with the same message - rebuilding our economy so it works better for everyone, protecting and expanding access to healthcare, revitalizing public education and increasing our vocational training, and confronting the climate change crisis with a clean energy economy.
We're going to talk about real issues Floridians are facing today, and what creative solutions we can develop in our state government. For example, when we talk about the Trump healthcare plan, we won't just talk about how bad President Trump's policies are -- we're going to talk about our plan to protect Floridians by ensuring women have access to no-cost contraception and people with pre-existing conditions don't face pricing discrimination or denials. **We 're going to give people something to vote for, instead of trying to tell them what they should vote against.**
But remember, this strategy will only work if we have the resources we need to compete statewide -- and that's why I'm asking, and I need to know now:
[**Will you sustain our campaign by making a contribution of whatever amount is meaningful to you?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3313/9e00690/4d24f1b8/24037aaf/1749865139/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170626-nb-nr")
**[CHIP IN $10](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3313/9e00690/4d24f1b8/24037aae/1749865139/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170626-button-10-nr&amount=10.00")**
**[CHIP IN $20](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3313/9e00690/4d24f1b8/24037aa1/1749865139/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170626-button-20-nr&amount=20.00")**
**[CHIP IN $50](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3313/9e00690/4d24f1b8/24037aa0/1749865139/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170626-button-50-nr&amount=50.00")**
**[CHIP IN $100](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3313/9e00690/4d24f1b8/24037aa3/1749865139/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170626-button-100-nr&amount=100.00")**
**[OR CHIP IN ANOTHER AMOUNT](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3313/9e00690/4d24f1b8/24037aa2/1749865139/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170626-button-another-nr")**
I'm looking forward to campaigning with you all across the state in this next year, and then some. Teamwork will make this dream work.
Bring it home,
Andrew

clean_body: Team -- Since the beginning of this campaign, we've known that our only path to victory is one we're willing to travel together. This isn't my campaign for Governor -- it's our campaign. And that's why I want to talk with you all today about our long-term campaign strategy -- because, as a member of our team, you deserve to know what our plan looks like. Our first election challenge will be our primary, which is a little over a year from today, in August 2018. By this time next June, we'll need to have a solid base of volunteers and organizers across the state. Our goal is to make sure we have people regularly calling voters and knocking doors in every single one of Florida's 67 counties. When I was an organizer in college, I used a method that we called "Arrive with Five" \-- where we'd encourage people to not only show up to vote, but to make sure they show up with five of their friends, too. That's a strategy (and a good tradition) that I would like to continue with our campaign. We're going to be up against self-funders, well-known names, and a lot of cynics and critics. And that's one of the reasons why we're starting these efforts so early -- because it takes time to build a grassroots movement and a ground game that can take on the special interests. There are no shortcuts -- this is a victory that we are going to have to earn every single day over the next year. This is a victory we cannot achieve alone. "" Before we start opening satellite campaign offices across the state, we need to know that we'll consistently have the resources we need to pay rent, purchase supplies like water and snacks for volunteers, and print campaign literature for us to hand out at the doors. We're going to run a lean and smart campaign -- please try not to tease us for our plastic picnic-table desks -- but it will still be expensive to reach out to as many voters as we need to in a state as big as Florida. On this campaign, there isn't any secret strategy to focus on one group of Floridians based on where they live or what they look like. We're going to run this campaign in all 67 counties with the same message - rebuilding our economy so it works better for everyone, protecting and expanding access to healthcare, revitalizing public education and increasing our vocational training, and confronting the climate change crisis with a clean energy economy. We're going to talk about real issues Floridians are facing today, and what creative solutions we can develop in our state government. For example, when we talk about the Trump healthcare plan, we won't just talk about how bad President Trump's policies are -- we're going to talk about our plan to protect Floridians by ensuring women have access to no-cost contraception and people with pre-existing conditions don't face pricing discrimination or denials. We 're going to give people something to vote for, instead of trying to tell them what they should vote against. But remember, this strategy will only work if we have the resources we need to compete statewide -- and that's why I'm asking, and I need to know now: "" "" "" "" "" "" I'm looking forward to campaigning with you all across the state in this next year, and then some. Teamwork will make this dream work. Bring it home, Andrew "" Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Heartbreaking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:

[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c26f7/7377f838/3694c73b/51fb16a5/254769910/VEsH/ "http://www.joeforindiana.com/")

| | Peter,

After weeks of secret negotiations, Senate Republicans just released the text of their bill to repeal the Affordable Care Act, and frankly, it's heartbreaking.

Among other things, this bill would:
* Gut Medicaid, which covers about half of all births in the United States;
* Cause seniors' premiums to skyrocket;
* Put addiction treatment programs out of reach for the people who need them most.

Babies. Seniors. Moms and dads -- these are the everyday folks I came to the Senate to fight for, and a plan that decimates their coverage to fund tax breaks for the ultra-rich will _never, ever_ get my vote.

I am willing to work with anyone who has good ideas for how to lower premiums, expand coverage, and improve the ACA -- but this isn't the way.

Peter, the Republicans are already campaigning to replace me with someone who will be a rubber stamp for their heartless slash-and-burn agenda, and I need your help.

**Can you chip in to my campaign so that I can stay in the Senate fighting for you?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

The GOP's methodical campaign to sabotage the Affordable Care Act is already having real consequences for people in Indiana. On Wednesday, two of the biggest insurance providers in the state announced that they were pulling out of our exchange because of the volatility and uncertainty that Congressional Republicans and the Trump Administration have intentionally created.

Now, they're trying to rush through this terrible bill without so much as a single committee hearing and inflict even more damage.

**Being a Democrat from a conservative state who is fighting the GOP 's repeal efforts tooth and nail has put a huge target on my back. I'm okay with that -- but I need your help to defend against the barrage of attacks.**

[**Let 's fight this together: Chip in to my campaign right now so I can stay in the Senate and keep fighting for Indiana.**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c26f7/7377f838/3694c73b/51fb16a3/254769910/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-hip?refcode=20170626resend&amount=25")

Thank you,
Joe
